expectations: Breaks the plan into vertical slices, not horizontal layers
Each slice is labeled HITL or AFK
Presents the breakdown and quizzes the user before publishing
Asks about granularity, dependencies, and HITL/AFK classification
Fetches issue #5 from the tracker and reads its full body and comments
Published issues use the issue body template with Parent, What to build, Acceptance criteria, Blocked by sections
Publishes issues in dependency order so blockers appear first
Does not close or modify the parent issue #5

files: open issue #3 of type Milestone: v1.0 launch
expectations: Queries for Milestone-type issues via gh api repos/.../issues?type=Milestone before creating any issue — does NOT use gh milestone list or --milestone flag
Links newly created issues as sub-issues of the Milestone-type parent issue via sub-issues API
Sets issue type on each created issue via gh api PATCH (type field), because gh issue create has no --type flag

files: issue #12 is an epic for the notification system
expectations: After creating each issue, calls GitHub sub-issues API to link it as sub-issue of #12
For issues blocked by another newly created issue, links blocked as sub-issue of blocker
Sub-issue linkage failure is non-blocking — prints warning and continues
